lines.speaker: FIRST WITCH
lines.text: When shall we three meet again

lines.speaker: FIRST WITCH
lines.text: In thunder, lightning, or in rain?

lines.speaker: SECOND WITCH
lines.text: When the battle’s lost and won.

lines.speaker: THIRD WITCH
lines.text: That will be ere the set of sun.

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Fair is foul, and foul is fair:

lines.speaker: ALL
lines.text: Hover through the fog and filthy air.

stage_directions.text: Thunder and lightning. Enter three Witches.

glosses.definition: tumult; uproar — the chaos of battle
open, uncultivated moorland, typical of the Scottish highlands
the First Witch's grey cat, her familiar spirit
the Second Witch's familiar, a toad

glosses.phrase: Fair is foul, and foul is fair
glosses.definition: the play's central paradox: moral categories will invert; appearances will deceive

glosses.phrase: Hover through the fog and filthy air
glosses.definition: the witches supernaturally pass through the thick air; the line establishes the play's atmosphere of metaphysical disorder

annotations.title: Thirteen lines that set the weather
annotations.body:
The shortest opening in Shakespeare. Thunder, three witches, a paradox ('Fair is foul, and foul is fair'), and an exit. Nothing is explained; the audience is handed an atmosphere and told that moral categories are about to invert.

The scene is a tonal overture rather than an exposition. Compare the deliberate exposition of *Hamlet* (the ghost, the watch, the state of Denmark) with this. *Macbeth* does not argue its weather; it declares it. The three witches will not appear again until line 32 of 1.3 — but the paradox they hand the audience is already installed, waiting for Macbeth's unwitting echo when he enters the play saying 'So foul and fair a day I have not seen.'
sources: L. C. Knights, 'How Many Children Had Lady Macbeth?' (1933)

trials.prompt: The Witches' line 'Fair is foul, and foul is fair' does what for the play?
options: Establishes a paradox about moral inversion that Macbeth will unwittingly echo in 1.3

trials.prompt: Where do the Witches plan to meet Macbeth?
options: On the heath, after the battle ends
